data.title: James Wynn Opens Long Bitcoin Position Again
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Lookonchain monitoring, James Wynn has just opened a long position in Bitcoin again. The current position size is 46 million US dollars, the opening price is 105,560.2 US dollars, and the liquidation price is 102,550 US dollars.

data.title: James Wynn transferred 344.7 ETH to Binance for sale and then transferred 500,000 USDC to Hyperliquid to open a BTC long order
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst Yu Jin, James Wynn collected 344.7 ETH (US$900,000) through multiple addresses in the past half hour and transferred them to Binance for sale, and then withdrew 500,000 USDC from Binance to Hyperliquid to open a BTC long order.
He currently holds more BTC and more PEPE, with positions:
40x more 437 BTC, worth US$46.1 million, opening price US$105,560, liquidation price US$102,713;
10x more 1.01 billion kPEPE, worth US$13.02 million, opening price US$0.0129, liquidation price US$0.0116.

data.title: Fed May View Inflation Report as Calm Before the Storm, Analyst Suggests
data.description: According to Odaily, Fitch analyst Olu Sonola has indicated in a report that the Federal Reserve might interpret the moderate personal consumption expenditure inflation report as a 'calm before the storm.' He noted that the Fed is likely to continue its wait-and-see approach unless there is a significant decline in consumer spending and a rapid increase in unemployment. Sonola remarked, 'This report demonstrates that American consumers remain resilient.'

data.title: LBank has launched SOLACE U-margined perpetual contracts
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, LBank has launched the SOLACE U-based perpetual contract at 15:50 (SGT) on May 30, 2025, with a maximum leverage of 50 times.

data.title: Fitch: PCE report is the calm before the storm in the eyes of the Fed
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Fitch analyst Olu Sonola pointed out in a report that the Federal Reserve may regard the mild personal consumption expenditure inflation report as "the calm before the storm." He wrote: The Fed will continue to wait for the storm to come-unless consumer spending shrinks sharply and the unemployment rate rises rapidly. Sonola said: "This report shows that American consumers are still resilient." (Jinshi)

data.title: More than a dozen securities firms held intensive conference calls within two days to focus on "stable currency"
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: From May 29 to May 30, more than a dozen brokerage telephone conferences were held with stablecoins as the keyword, involving more than a dozen brokerages in the industry. In the past few days, many stablecoin concept stocks have also performed strongly. According to previous reports, the Legislative Council of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region recently passed the "Stablecoin Bill", and the impact and opportunities of the passage of this bill on various links and participants in the stablecoin industry have recently attracted much attention from brokerages.

data.title: Suilend: The second season SEND points and STEAMM points snapshot has been taken
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Sui ecological lending protocol Suilend posted on the X platform that the second quarter SEND points and STEAMM points snapshots have been taken, and the second quarter application details will be released next week.

data.title: Stablecoin Industry Gains Attention Amid Legislative Developments
data.description: According to Odaily, numerous brokerage firms held over ten conference calls focused on stablecoins from May 29 to May 30. These discussions involved more than a dozen industry brokers. During this period, several stocks related to stablecoin concepts showed strong performance. Recently, the Legislative Council of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region passed the Stablecoin Ordinance Bill. This legislative development has drawn significant attention from brokers, highlighting its potential impact and opportunities for various stakeholders within the stablecoin industry.

data.title: PCE inflation drops to 2.1%, lowest level in 8 months. What’s the Fed next move?
data.description:
PCE inflation cooled to 2. 1% in April, the lowest it’s been in eight months, even though President Donald Trump fired off new tariffs earlier in the month. That’s what the Commerce Department confirmed on Friday. Monthly inflation only climbed by 0. 1%, which matched the Dow Jones estimate, while the yearly number slid slightly below expectations […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/feds-pce-inflation-drops-to-2-1/

data.title: Stripe co-founder: A large part of the future payment volume will be in the form of stablecoins
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Joh Collison, co-founder and president of global payment company Stripe, said in an interview with Bloomberg that the company has had early discussions with banks on the integration of stablecoins, and said, "A large part of our future payment volume will be in the form of stablecoins, and banks are very interested in how to integrate stablecoins into their product systems. Banks will not ignore this, nor will they regard it as a temporary craze." (CoinDesk)

data.title: Potential Inflation Concerns Arise Amid Tariff Changes
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Nick Timiraos, known as the 'Fed's Mouthpiece,' highlighted two potential issues in the recently released inflation report. Analysts predict that with the implementation of 'Liberation Day' tariffs, the rate of increase in commodity prices will accelerate in May, particularly in June. Despite a cooling trend in inflation by mid-2024, the year-on-year comparison base is not advantageous, as evidenced by the U.S. core PCE price index recording an annual rate of 2.5% in April, marking the lowest since March 2021.

data.title: U.S. Consumer Spending Slows in April Amid Economic Concerns
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data indicates that U.S. consumer spending slowed in April following the strongest month of consumption since early 2023, while inflation remained moderate, aligning with the broader economic slowdown.

The core Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index in the U.S. rose by 2.5% year-over-year in April, marking the smallest annual increase in over four years. This data highlights underlying anxiety among many American consumers after the weakest consumption quarter in nearly two years. Despite high tariffs on imported goods not yet widely impacting prices, consumer sentiment has significantly declined, and personal financial outlooks are at historic lows.

Another report released on Friday showed a significant narrowing of the U.S. goods trade deficit in April, driven by the largest recorded drop in import volumes, with goods imports falling by 19.8%, the largest decrease on record.

data.title: Federal Reserve's June Rate Decision Probabilities Analyzed
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the CME's FedWatch tool indicates a 5.4% probability of a 25 basis point rate cut by the Federal Reserve in June, while the likelihood of maintaining the current rate stands at 94.6%.
